title: Uncut Gems
logline: A charismatic New York jewelry dealer and compulsive gambler bets everything on a rare opal and a basketball game, spiraling through increasingly desperate schemes as debts, family, and dangerous creditors close in around him.
protagonist: Howard Nagy (Ratner), a compulsive gambling jewelry dealer in his early 50s who constantly chases the next big score while juggling debts, family obligations, and risky business deals
antagonistOrConflict: Howard's self-destructive addiction to gambling and high-stakes deals, manifested through loan shark Jim and his enforcers who demand payment, plus Howard's inability to stop doubling down on risk
tone: Relentlessly anxious, frenetic, darkly comedic with moments of domestic tragedy; a pressure-cooker atmosphere where every scene escalates the protagonist's desperation
themes: The price of living beyond one's means
Masculinity, ego, and the gambler's fatal optimism

actBreaks.description: Howard meets with loan shark Anthony who refuses to extend credit, establishing the ticking clock of debt that will drive the rest of the story.

validation.ruling: Scene #32 shows Howard making the massive 5-team parlay bet with his brother-in-law Anthony using the $65k, committing irreversibly to his plan and raising stakes. This fundamentally shifts the story from setup into escalating complications, functioning as a clear act break at 33.6%.

actBreaks.description: The auction house refuses to auction Howard's opal, forcing him to retrieve the gem and pivot to an even riskier final gambit.

validation.ruling: Scene #84 reveals the opal is not the precious stone Howard claimed—a devastating revelation that collapses his entire scheme at the auction house. This major twist at 68.9% forces Howard into the final act of desperate survival and represents an irreversible plot shift into the climactic sequence.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates on pure kinetic energy rather than traditional three-act mechanics. It's structured like a tightening noose: each scene adds a new complication or debt while offering Howard a tantalizing glimpse of salvation that he immediately leverages into further risk. The first act establishes Howard's ecosystem of chaos—family trouble in Miami, the arrival of the opal, Amare's interest, mounting debts. The second act watches him juggle all these elements with manic confidence, getting locked in the school, beaten in the limo, yet always talking his way forward. The final act strips away all illusions as the gem fails at auction and Howard locks his creditors inside his own store, betting everything on a single game. The structure mirrors gambling itself: early wins, mounting losses, one final all-in bet.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deliberately suffocating with an average scene length of 1.5 pages, creating a staccato rhythm of constant interruptions and escalations. The first 40 pages sprint through multiple cities and crises (Miami furniture theft, Vegas gambling, Ray Forcaster meeting) before settling into the pressure-cooker of the Diamond District. Scene 25 through Scene 31 form the script's first major set piece—Amare's visit and the opal viewing—stretched across 14 pages of mounting tension. The school sequence (scenes 51-64) provides brief physical comedy before the limo confrontation darkens the tone. The final 15 pages lock everyone in the jewelry store vestibule while the basketball game plays out, creating unbearable tension through spatial confinement and split-screen urgency between Nadav at Foxwoods and Howard watching the game.

incitingIncident.description: Sadie helps a white customer select a VVS2 diamond in a channel setting while Howard arrives, confronts loan shark Larry about impossible payment demands, and shoves cash into his assistant Dina's pocket to send her to Barney's while lying about hospital visits and claiming he hasn't touched $5,000 in a month.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - SHOWROOM - CONTINUOUS

validation.ruling: Scene #25 is Howard's first meeting with Demany and the basketball players regarding the opal, establishing the central conflict and goal of selling/leveraging the stone. This shatters his ordinary dealing routine and forces him into irreversible high-stakes action that drives the entire plot.

midpoint.description: The next morning, an unkempt Howard argues on the phone about a basketball bet, insisting he had five grand on the Celtics and threatening to sue when the operator denies any record of his wager.
midpoint.sceneHeading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- BACKROOM - MORNING

validation.ruling: Scene #64 is a violent confrontation but not the true midpoint shift. Scene #74 (p68, 57.1%) shows Howard discovering the opal is missing the morning after lending it to Amare—this is the point of no return where he shifts from proactive dealing to desperate reactive survival mode, raising stakes massively as his entire scheme collapses.

climax.description: When Nadav doesn't answer, Howard begs Jim to wait until after the game, but Jim coldly says goodbye and the three men exit to the vestibule, where Howard locks them in by having Sadie not buzz the second door.
climax.sceneHeading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - SHOWROOM - CONTINUOUS

validation.ruling: Scene #114 is the ultimate confrontation where Howard locks his creditors in the vestibule while refusing to let them leave until his bet plays out. This is the highest tension, demonstrates his compulsive character arc, and resolves the main story question of whether his gambling will destroy him—perfectly positioned at 93.3%.

scenes.heading: EXT. OPAL MINE - ETHIOPIA - DAY
scenes.summary: White foremen and black miners violently clash in a riot outside an Ethiopian opal mine.

scenes.heading: INT. OPAL MINE - ETHIOPIA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Two black miners sneak into the mine during the chaos, find a hidden t-shirt in a wall crevice, and extract a large dirt clump using a screwdriver.

scenes.heading: INT. UNIVERSAL BOXING GYM, QUEENS, NY - RING -NIGHT
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in the extraction.

scenes.heading: INT. UNIVERSAL BOXING GYM, QUEENS, NY - LOCKERROOM - AFTER
scenes.summary: Nadav applies cologne and adorns himself with gold chains and a Rolex watch from his underwear drawer while Arthur smokes by the window and takes note of the jewelry.

scenes.heading: INT. NADAV’S APARTMENT, SUNNYSIDE QUEENS - BEDROOM - AFTER
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in the extraction.

scenes.heading: INT. GLAZZ NIGHT CLUB - AFTER
scenes.summary: Arthur and Dev flirt with girls at a bar while Nadav, drunk and one of few white people in the club, clings on awkwardly.

scenes.heading: INT. NADAV’S APARTMENT, BUILDING HALLWAY - 12AM
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in the extraction.

scenes.heading: INT. NADAV’S APARTMENT - AFTER
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in the extraction.

scenes.heading: INT. NADAV’S APARTMENT, SUNNYSIDE QUEENS - AFTER
scenes.summary: Nadav discovers his apartment ransacked with TV, stereo, and jewelry stolen, bursts into tears, and desperately calls Howard who arrives at 2 AM and gives him $40 for a cab to stay at the house.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAGUARDIA AIRPORT - 2 AM
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in the extraction.

scenes.heading: INT. MIAMI HIGH RISE APARTMENT BUILDING - 6 AM
scenes.summary: Howard answers his phone while Carmelle and his sister-in-law Gabi yell at him in the background, gives Nadav $40 to take a cab, and is told to straighten things out tomorrow.

scenes.heading: INT. GABI NAGY’S APARTMENT, LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in the extraction.

scenes.heading: INT. GABI NAGY’S APARTMENT, BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Carmelle violently grabs Howard and shows him the empty apartment with only folding chairs remaining, shoves a note about a missing $17,000 gold sofa set in his face, and Howard locks himself in the bathroom while she screams invectives through the door.
scenes.turningPointNote: Howard's theft of his brother's furniture is revealed, establishing the depths of his self-destructive behavior and family betrayal.

scenes.heading: EXT. MIAMI STREET, NEAR BEACH. 7 AM
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in the extraction.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH, SOUTH BEACH MIAMI - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard tries to calm Gabi after the furniture incident, then walks frantically on the phone with Jim the loan shark, desperately promising to pay $175,000 he's holding in cash before his phone dies, then plays with a jogger's dog on the beach.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE. DAYTIME, 30,000 FEET.
scenes.summary: Howard sits in first class drinking Coke Zero and compulsively rips pages of luxury items from magazines.

scenes.heading: INT. MIRAGE CASINO, LAS VEAGAS. CHIP EXCHANGE - DAYTIME
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in the extraction.

scenes.heading: INT. MIRAGE CASINO, LAS VEAGAS. SPORTS BOOK - AFTER
scenes.summary: Howard places a three-game college football parlay for $40,000 at the sports book.

scenes.heading: INT. MIRAGE CASINO, LAS VEGAS. NEAR SPORTS BOOK.
scenes.summary: Howard plays blackjack alone buying all seven hands with $1,000 chips while waiting for east coast games, splits pairs, doubles down, and orders a whole bottle of sparkling water from the waitress.

scenes.heading: INT. BELLAGIO HOTEL, LAS VEGAS. PRESIDENTIAL SUITE. EVENING.
scenes.summary: Howard visits champion boxer Ray Forcaster in his palatial suite to retrieve jewelry, sees his pinky ring on a bodyguard and gets it back, pitches a jewel-encrusted Rolls Royce, tells Ray Jews and Blacks are the same people, and Ray impulsively throws five bundles of cash at Howard after Howard says it'll come back to him.

scenes.heading: EXT. TETERBOROUGH PRIVATE AIRPORT - ARRIVALS - EARLY MORNING.
scenes.summary: Ray refuses to return Howard's day-date watches, revealing FakeWatchBusta already called him out twice for wearing fakes, and Howard admits Ray could sell them at discount for ten thousand.

scenes.heading: EXT. DIAMOND DISTRICT - 46TH STREET BTW 6TH AND 5TH - 11 AM
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in the extraction.

scenes.heading: INT. 47TH STREET JEWELRY ARCADE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard guzzles water in Yussi's car, asks if anyone was waiting at Gems, learns Dina called Yussi fifteen times with increasingly desperate texts, and tells Yussi to drive.

scenes.heading: EXT. 47TH ST BETWEEN 6TH & 5TH AVE, THE DIAMOND DISTRICT - AFTER
scenes.summary: Howard and Yussi cut through the crowded jewelry arcade and Howard stops at Joshua's booth, calling him a disgusting pig for getting crumbs on his merchandise.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - SHOWROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Sadie helps a white customer select a VVS2 diamond in a channel setting while Howard arrives, confronts loan shark Larry about impossible payment demands, and shoves cash into his assistant Dina's pocket to send her to Barney's while lying about hospital visits and claiming he hasn't touched $5,000 in a month.
scenes.turningPointNote: Howard juggles multiple debts and lies while maintaining his jewelry business facade, establishing the crushing financial pressure driving his desperation.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- BACKROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in the extraction.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- SHOWROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard gives Dina cash for Barney's to pacify her, argues with loan shark Larry who demands $80,000 and reveals he went to the hospital to check Howard's lie, then Howard shows NBA player Amare Stoudemire a ruby with a loupe.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - BACKROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Amare rejects the ruby and puts down the loupe disinterested, then Nadav arrives with a large styrofoam crate and Howard grows excited.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - SHOWROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in the extraction.

scenes.heading: INT. INSIDE THE GEMSTONE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in the extraction.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - SHOWROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard cracks open the crate, slices through vacuum-sealed fish to find a wet black plastic bag containing a large 600-carat uncut Black Opal from the African mine, examines it with his loupe, and excitedly presents it to Amare as something from an untapped mine.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Ethiopian Black Opal arrives, the MacGuffin that will drive Howard's ultimate gamble and downfall.

scenes.heading: INT. BORATTA’S RESTAURANT - EARLY EVENING
scenes.summary: Amare gives Howard his word as his bodyguard warns Howard to pray Amare plays well tonight, then Howard confronts bookie Anthony at his restaurant table with $65,000 cash and a Rolex, demanding to place a five-team parlay despite Anthony refusing to take his action anymore.

scenes.heading: INT. BAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in the extraction.

scenes.heading: EXT. 3RD AVENUE. AFTER.
scenes.summary: Howard watches Amare's mythical 29-point performance on TV in a crowded sports bar, checks Instagram to see 549 new followers and a comment calling him a sweaty fatass JEWler, then claps like a seal when the announcer mentions Amare still has 28 minutes left to play.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST 55TH STREET - AFTER
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in the extraction.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARMENT BUILDING - JUST AFTER
scenes.summary: Howard realizes he's standing in front of Wollensky's steakhouse, then approaches a sleeping homeless man, places a container of food beside him, and peels off several hundred-dollar bills to leave with him.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT- KITCHEN/LIVING ROOM - AFTER
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in the extraction.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT. THE BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in the extraction.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT- BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard tells the concierge to say they left town if anyone comes by, kicks off his shoes, calls for Sadie with takeout, walks through his garish apartment filled with infomercial gadgets, finds the messy bedroom, and discovers Sadie in the bathtub.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT- HOWARD’S BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in the extraction.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT- KITCHEN/LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard complains about cigarette smell and organizes toiletries around the sink while Sadie asks for privacy, then he exits and examines suspicious fingerprint-like smudge marks on the bedroom mirror before unbuttoning his shirt.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT- HOWARD’S BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard leaves a voicemail for Demany demanding the gem back by 9 AM because people are waiting, hangs up, closes the closet door exposing topless Sadie, and they argue about the messy apartment while Amare appears on the bedroom TV.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- BACKROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in the extraction.

scenes.heading: EXT. MIDTOWN STREET - DIAMOND DISTRICT- AFTER
scenes.summary: Howard waits at his desk staring at the phone set to speaker while Elan, Nadav, and Yussi work around him, then the call goes to voicemail at 10:27 AM.

scenes.heading: EXT. BED-STUY PROJECTS - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Loan shark Larry confronts Howard about his $65,000 parlay and demands the $80,000 owed, so Howard unbuckles and gives Larry his $28,000 Rolex watch that can sell used for $15,000, then dismisses a call from Gabi.

scenes.heading: INT. DEMANY’S APARTMENT
scenes.summary: Howard confronts hungover Demany at his apartment door, accusing him of ignoring over twenty calls, and demands the gem back because it's sold with people waiting, but Demany casually reveals he hasn't been using his phone and asks what's going on.
scenes.turningPointNote: Howard discovers Demany doesn't have the gem, triggering the desperate chase that will dominate the story's final act.

scenes.heading: INT. DEMANY’S LEXUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Howard demands Demany call Amare about the million-dollar gem, but Demany insists Amare is at practice and won't answer, so Howard convinces Demany to drive to practice by offering him the ruby and reminding him about the chain he's owed.

scenes.heading: INT. KNICKS PRACTICE FACILITY - AFTER
scenes.summary: Howard and Demany drive in Demany's red Lexus with loud hip-hop playing while Howard brags about hitting a five-way parlay and asks invasive questions about whether Demany sleeps with his own girls or gets Amare's run-off.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY STREET - AFTER
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in the extraction.

scenes.heading: INT. LACE STRIP CLUB - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Security stops Howard at the practice facility entrance, Demany walks away claiming he doesn't know Howard, leaving Howard yelling after him while offering the security guard two hundred dollars for three minutes, but the guard ignores him and deals with the next person in line, then Howard rushes down a city street and enters a strip club smiling to meet Anthony and collect his parlay winnings.

scenes.heading: EXT. SCHOOL BUILDING, QUEENS, NY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Anthony returns Howard's Rolex watch box to him outside a Queens school building at night.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL AUDITORIUM - AFTER
scenes.summary: Howard sits with Dina and their sons in a school auditorium watching Marcel's Snow White play, joking with Eddie by pulling a shoulder-tap prank on a stranger.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL AUDITORIUM - 10 ROWS BACK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard spots loan shark enforcers Tuna and Robert staring at him from ten rows back in the auditorium.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard confronts Tuna and Robert in a school hallway, calling Jim to protest their presence at his daughter's school.

scenes.heading: INT. ANOTHER SCHOOL HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard picks up his pace and walks in the opposite direction as Robert and Tuna pursue him through another hallway.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL AUDITORIUM - BACK STAGE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard frantically tries locked classroom doors and flees backstage where his daughter Marcel greets him before he escapes through opposite doors.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL STAIRWELL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard descends to the basement and navigates a labyrinthine maze of dark corridors to reach the boiler room.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL BOILER ROOM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Howard squeezes behind the hot boiler, burning his arm, as Robert and Tuna search for him with Tuna insisting he's in there.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL AUDITORIUM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: On stage, Marcel as Snow White accepts the poisoned apple from the Queen and takes a bite after the Queen eats first to prove it's safe.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL BOILER ROOM - MUCH LATER
scenes.summary: Much later, Howard emerges from behind the boiler and gropes around trying classroom doors before returning to the basement.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL HALLWAY - AFTER
scenes.summary: Howard pushes on sublevel windows until finding a loose one, climbs through barely fitting, and emerges in the playground.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL BASEMENT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard runs to the locked gate and scales the fence, ripping his pants in the process.

scenes.heading: EXT. SCHOOL PLAYGROUND - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The moment Howard's feet touch ground, a limo pulls up and a guy runs out and whacks him in the back with a mini baseball bat.

scenes.heading: INT. LIMOUSINE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Jim forces Howard into the limo where he sits between Tuna and Robert, begging to leave because he's late, but Jim insists they go to Greenhouse nightclub together to meet Amare Stoudemire.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jim forces Howard to bring his loan shark crew to meet Amare, intertwining Howard's gambling debts with his big-score opportunity.

scenes.heading: EXT. GREENHOUSE NIGHT CLUB - AFTER THE CAR RIDE
scenes.summary: Howard, disheveled and missing hair, palms a hundred-dollar bill to the bouncer at Greenhouse nightclub.

scenes.heading: INT. GREENHOUSE NIGHT CLUB - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard navigates through the crowded club, approaching Amare's bodyguards and asking cool young guys Kev and Paris where Demany and Sadie are located.

scenes.heading: INT. GREENHOUSE NIGHT CLUB - BANQUETTE
scenes.summary: Paris tells Howard that Sadie has bladder problems and is in the bathroom, causing Howard's smile to recede as he shouts to Jim he'll be right back.

scenes.heading: INT. GREENHOUSE NIGHT CLUB - BATHROOM HALLWAY
scenes.summary: Two big men push Howard against the wall when he bangs on the bathroom door until Privelej emerges wasted with unbuckled pants, revealing Sadie inside barely able to hold herself up.

scenes.heading: EXT. GREENHOUSE NIGHT CLUB - BACK ALLEY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard drags Sadie out while Privelej, confused and angry, calls Howard a faggot before Demany's crew pushes them into the back alley.

scenes.heading: INT. CAB - AFTER
scenes.summary: In the cab, Howard has a fifteen-second power cry while the wasted Sadie repeatedly tries to press the off button on the taxi TV.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOWARD’S BUILDING
scenes.summary: The cab pulls up to Howard's apartment building where a doorman helps Sadie out while Howard stays inside and directs the driver to continue downtown.

scenes.heading: EXT. DIAMOND DISTRICT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Howard walks down the deserted 47th street Diamond District at night.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - BACKROOM - 145AM
scenes.summary: Howard flips on the lights at KMA Gems, throws his jacket on the desk, and sets up a makeshift bed on the floor with a blanket and pillow.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- BACKROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: The next morning, an unkempt Howard argues on the phone about a basketball bet, insisting he had five grand on the Celtics and threatening to sue when the operator denies any record of his wager.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- SHOWROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Yussi threatens to call his sister Dina and tell her everything about Sadie, Sara, and the money, then storms out slamming doors when Howard calls his bluff.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- BACKROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard refuses to speak with Sadie when she calls upset, sarcastically asking if she's taken Demany's calls, then hangs up when a casino operator claims no record of his bet.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - SHOWROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Amare arrives but the interior door won't open despite Joani buzzing repeatedly, forcing Howard to kneel down and discover the lock is bent from Yussi's earlier slamming.

scenes.heading: EXT. 47TH STREET BETWEEN 6TH AND 5TH, DIAMOND DISTRICT - AFTER
scenes.summary: Howard calls a locksmith and then Elan, ordering him to run to the hardware store and buy a sledgehammer.

scenes.heading: EXT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - OUT WINDOW - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard leans out a window, instructing Elan on the street below to tie a sledgehammer to twine, nearly cracking a third-floor window when it catches on concrete molding during the haul-up.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - SHOWROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Howard uses the sledgehammer to smash the jammed door open, freeing the sweat-covered Amare who asks to speak privately in Howard's office.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- BACKROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Amare explains the opal's spiritual importance to him and offers $175,000 cash, but Howard insists it's worth half a million and will be auctioned, though he promises Amare first right of refusal.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- SHOWROOM
scenes.summary: Amare asks to hold the opal until Sunday after his road games, but Howard refuses despite Amare's repeated requests, causing the basketball star to walk back to the showroom.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- VESTIBULE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard opens the hallway door to find a puffy-faced Sadie who he refuses to listen to, pushing her out of the elevator when she tries to follow him.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRISTIES AUCTION HOUSE BACK ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: At Christie's, appraiser Aren examines the opal and tells Howard they'll withdraw it from auction, but Howard insists he'll deal with Steven and asks if they can do the appraisal video.

scenes.heading: INT. GOOEY’S - PARK AVENUE APARTMENT - DINING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At Gooey's formal Shabbat dinner, Howard's cousin violently throws a piece of challah bread at his bratty son Edmond for not sitting properly, while Howard's family watches in awkward silence.

scenes.heading: INT. GOOEY’S - PARK AVENUE APARTMENT - HALLWAY TO DINING ROOM
scenes.summary: Howard gets up from the dinner table despite Dina's disapproving look, immediately answering a call from Sadie in the hallway.

scenes.heading: INT. GOOEY’S - PARK AVENUE APARTMENT - CHILDRENS BEDROOM
scenes.summary: In a children's bedroom decorated like a playground, Sadie tearfully apologizes over the phone saying she doesn't remember what happened, while Howard coldly tells her she sounds like television.

scenes.heading: INT. GOOEY’S - PARK AVENUE APARTMENT - DEN - DURING
scenes.summary: Howard demands Sadie move out by 10pm that night while sitting in Gooey's den, then returns to watch ESPN basketball recaps where he sees Amare wearing the opal during a game, realizing the stone was never returned.
scenes.turningPointNote: Howard discovers Amare took the opal without permission, wearing it during a televised game, escalating Howard's crisis as his auction centerpiece is missing.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD AND DINA’S FAMILY CAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Howard drives his BMW SUV with his family inside, checking the clock to see it's 10:15 as Dina questions why he's going down Fifth Avenue.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOWARD’S BUILDING - AFTER
scenes.summary: Howard pulls up to his building and tells the doorman he's just popping up for a second while Dina and the children watch him enter.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT. LIVING ROOM/KITCHENETTE - AFTER
scenes.summary: Howard enters his apartment and finds Sadie crying on the couch, announcing he'll pack for her instead.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT - BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard violently throws Sadie's clothes and shoes into a suitcase, ranting about her fucking black guys in club bathrooms before pulling out an entire dresser drawer and dumping it.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT - LIVING ROOM/KITCHENETTE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard drags the overstuffed suitcase through the apartment while Sadie begs him to stop, repeatedly screaming that she loves him.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT - BUILDING HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Howard shoves the suitcase into the hallway, violently slamming the apartment door in Sadie's face as she cries outside.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD AND DINA’S FAMILY CAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Howard gets back in the car where Dina icily asks if he got what he wanted, and he silently starts the engine and drives away.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET IN SUBURBAN QUEENS, FOREST HILLS - AFTER
scenes.summary: The BMW drives down a tree-lined street of garish suburban Queens homes and pulls into the driveway.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD AND DINA’S FAMILY CAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dina orders Howard to pull the recycling bin out before coming inside, and he slowly drags it to the curb while lights turn on illuminating his movements.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRISTIE’S AUCTION HOUSE - AUCTION FLOOR - AFTER
scenes.summary: At Christie's auction house, an Indian auctioneer sells ruby necklaces while Gooey sits in the front row, and the Ethiopian opal sells for $190,000 to phone bidder number eight.
scenes.turningPointNote: The opal auctions for far less than Howard's half-million projection, meaning he owes Gooey money he doesn't have and his big score has collapsed.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRISTIE’S AUCTION HOUSE - HALLWAY - AFTER
scenes.summary: In the Christie's hallway, an livid Gooey confronts Howard about the low price, and Howard promises to wire the money back once it clears, offering to cover Christie's twelve percent commission himself.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRISTIE’S AUCTION HOUSE - LLOYD’S OFFICE
scenes.summary: Christie's manager Lloyd stops Howard and Gooey in the hallway to speak with them.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHRISTIE’S AUCTION HOUSE - 49TH ST - AFTER
scenes.summary: Howard and Gooey are banned from Christie's for shill bidding, then immediately confronted by Jim and Robert outside with the gem in hand.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT - BATHROOM - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Jim forces Howard to call Amare on speaker to confirm the $175K sale, but the call goes to voicemail and Howard leaves a desperate message offering the gem.

scenes.heading: INT. HOWARD’S APARTMENT - BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Sadie pranks Howard in the shower by repeatedly pouring shampoo on his head while he tries to rinse, frustrating him until he realizes it's her.

scenes.heading: EXT. 47TH STREET BETWEEN 6TH & 5TH, DIAMOND DISTRICT - NEXT DAY
scenes.summary: Howard speaks to Amare on the phone about the gem sale while Sadie caresses and undresses him, setting up a 4pm meeting for the next day.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - BACKROOM - AFTER
scenes.summary: Amare arrives with $175K in cash and gives Howard the leather bag, acknowledging Howard's auction manipulation but honoring his word to buy the gem.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - SHOWROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Amare exits with the gem into the showroom.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - BACKROOM
scenes.summary: Howard instructs Nadav to take all the cash to Foxwoods and place a two-way parlay bet on Amare's points/rebounds and the game spread, then sends him out secretly.
scenes.turningPointNote: Howard gambles everything instead of paying his debts, the ultimate act of self-destructive compulsion that sets the climax in motion.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - SHOWROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Amare says goodbye to Sadie in the showroom and Jim interrupts to greet him.

scenes.heading: EXT. 43RD AND 5TH AVENUE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Nadav hops into a Lincoln Town Car on the street.

scenes.heading: INT. LINCOLN TOWN CAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Nadav tries to hire the Lincoln driver to take him to Foxwoods but the driver refuses.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - BACKROOM - AFTER
scenes.summary: Jim and Robert discover the empty bag and Howard excitedly reveals he sent Nadav to bet all the money on a 4-to-1 parlay, offering Jim a cut of the winnings.

scenes.heading: EXT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - BACKROOM - OUT OF WINDOW
scenes.summary: Jim demands Howard call Nadav back with the cash, but when Howard refuses, Robert holds a gun to his face and they dangle him out the window until he agrees to call.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- BACKROOM
scenes.summary: Howard is pulled back inside and Jim forces him to dial Nadav's number on speakerphone while Howard trembles and tries to stall.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE - SHOWROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: When Nadav doesn't answer, Howard begs Jim to wait until after the game, but Jim coldly says goodbye and the three men exit to the vestibule, where Howard locks them in by having Sadie not buzz the second door.
scenes.turningPointNote: Howard traps his creditors in the vestibule, a desperate power move that escalates the situation to a point of no return.

scenes.heading: INT. LINCOLN TOWN CAR - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Howard calls Dina and lies about a gas leak to evacuate her from their home while Jim's men arrive outside and Jim realizes he's locked in.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOXWOODS CASINO, LEDYARD, CT. - 6:55PM.
scenes.summary: Nadav's car arrives at Foxwoods Casino.

scenes.heading: INT. FOXWOODS CASINO, LEDYARD, CT. - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Nadav runs into Foxwoods Casino.

scenes.heading: INT. FOXWOODS CASINO, LEDYARD, CT. - SPORTS BOOK
scenes.summary: Nadav finds the sports book and asks a stranger for directions.

scenes.heading: INT. KMA GEMS AND JEWELRY OFFICE- SHOWROOM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Nadav places the $175K parlay bet at Foxwoods while Howard sets up a TV in front of the locked vestibule where Jim, Robert, and Nico sit trapped and sweating.
